rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative radical cascade oxydifluoromethylation of N-acyl-(2-ene)anilines (olefinic amides) with sodium difluoromethanesulfinate to give difluoromethylated benzoxazines in an undivided cell.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of CF2HSO2Na (2a) on graphite anode to generate the difluoromethyl radical (with SO2 extrusion); a second anodic oxidation converts the cyclized benzyl radical to a benzyl cation. Cathode (Pt) reduces protons to H2.

key_intermediates.name: benzyl radical Int-1 from CF2H addition to alkene
benzyl cation Int-2 (after anodic oxidation of Int-1)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes CF2HSO2Na to •CF2H (loss of SO2), which adds to the alkene of the N-acyl olefinic amide to give a benzyl radical (Int-1); a second anodic oxidation generates a benzyl cation (Int-2) that is trapped by intramolecular nucleophilic attack of the amide oxygen to form the benzoxazine, while protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3aa formed only in trace at 1.0–3.0 equiv BHT; BHT–CF2H adduct 4 detected by EI-MS, supporting •CF2H formation.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction fully inhibited at 2.0–3.0 equiv TEMPO; TEMPO–CF2H adduct 5 detected by EI-MS; however putative TEMPO adduct of the benzyl radical (6) was not detected, and self-coupling products 7/8 of Int-1 were not observed.

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry
probe_reagents.observation: Olefin 1a not oxidized up to 2.0 V (vs Ag/AgCl); 2a oxidized at ~0.75 V, indicating the cascade is initiated by oxidation of 2a rather than the alkene, and ruling out a radical–radical coupling pathway.

notes: A radical–radical coupling pathway between •CF2H and an anilide-derived radical was explicitly ruled out by CV (1a is not oxidized in the operating window). Absence of TEMPO-trapped benzyl radical (6) and self-coupling products (7,8) is rationalized by rapid further anodic oxidation of Int-1 to the cation Int-2 (radical–polar crossover) rather than radical termination.
